title: Fix OCR date extraction from uploaded medical report documents

requirements.text: Fix the date extraction logic in `frontend/src/lib/ocrExtraction.ts` so that the report date is reliably detected from uploaded medical report documents. Expand the regex patterns to cover common date formats found in medical reports, including but not limited to: 'DD/MM/YYYY', 'MM/DD/YYYY', 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'DD-MM-YYYY', 'DD MMM YYYY' (e.g., '12 Jan 2024'), 'MMM DD, YYYY' (e.g., 'Jan 12, 2024'), and label-prefixed formats such as 'Date:', 'Report Date:', 'Collection Date:', 'Sample Date:', 'Test Date:', and 'Date of Report:'. The extracted date must be returned as a valid ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) in the `ExtractedTestData` result and pre-populated in the date field of each extracted record displayed in the `UploadRecordForm` component.

quotes: date isnt picked from the document

acceptanceCriteria: Uploading a medical report PDF or image with a date in any of the supported formats results in the date field being correctly pre-populated in the review form.
Dates prefixed with labels like 'Date:', 'Report Date:', 'Collection Date:', 'Sample Date:', 'Test Date:', or 'Date of Report:' are correctly parsed.
Unlabelled dates in common formats (DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, DD MMM YYYY, MMM DD YYYY) are detected and returned.
The extracted date is converted to a valid ISO 8601 string (YYYY-MM-DD) before being set in the form state.
If no date is found, the date field remains empty (no crash or invalid value is set).
All previously passing OCR extraction tests for patient name and test categories continue to pass.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Changes must be limited to the OCR extraction utility and, if necessary, the UploadRecordForm component for wiring the date field.
nonGoals: Adding backend OCR processing — extraction remains client-side.
Changing the date storage format in the backend data model.
Altering any other OCR extraction fields (patient name, test categories, metric values).
